Italian Blue Cheese Pizza with Smoked Speck

english: Blue Cheese and Smoked Ham Pizza
Italian Blue Cheese Pizza with Smoked Speck

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza food product class (FOODON_00002439). No specific Gorgonzola e Speck pizza subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific Gorgonzola and Speck variant exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 is the generic pizza concept. No specific Gorgonzola e Speck pizza concept exists; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Gorgonzola e Speck pizza, approximately 300 g, as typically served at an Italian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with cheese (SR Legacy, FDC ID 170720) and component ingredient data for Gorgonzola-style blue cheese (FDC ID 173414) and cured smoked pork (FDC ID 167903); values weighted and adapted for this topping combination.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry exists for Gorgonzola e Speck pizza. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pizza with cheese as the base (FDC ID 170720, ~266 kcal/100 g), adjusted upward for the higher-fat Gorgonzola DOP and smoked Speck toppings. Gorgonzola blue cheese (FDC ID 173414: ~353 kcal, 21.4 g protein, 28.7 g fat/100 g) and cured smoked pork/speck-type product (FDC ID 167903: ~337 kcal, 22 g protein, 27 g fat/100 g) were used as ingredient anchors. Final per-100 g values represent a weighted composite estimate for a pizza with these toppings at typical restaurant proportions (dough+sauce ~55%, mozzarella ~15%, Gorgonzola ~10%, Speck ~10%, onions ~10%).

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI ~50, referenced against glucose).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Gorgonzola e Speck pizza. The closest approved analogous food is cheese pizza (thin crust) with a published GI of approximately 50 (glucose reference) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The high fat and protein content from Gorgonzola and Speck would further attenuate postprandial glucose response, so GI 50 is a conservative upper-bound estimate. GL per serving = (50 × 27.5 × 300/100) / 100 = 41.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~680 mg/100 g; ~2040 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its very high sodium load from Gorgonzola DOP, Tiroler Speck, and mozzarella; a single serving may exceed the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) and high glycemic load per serving (~41 g) due to refined wheat dough base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions, as the refined-flour crust delivers a substantial glycemic load per serving despite a moderate GI; the high fat and protein from Gorgonzola and Speck partially blunt the postprandial glucose rise but do not eliminate it.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.8 g/100 g) and high sodium (~680 mg/100 g) from blue cheese and cured smoked pork
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this pizza due to its combined burden of saturated fat from Gorgonzola and mozzarella and high sodium from Speck and cheese, both of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gorgonzola e Speck is an Italian-style pizza topped with Gorgonzola D.O.P. (a protected-designation-of-origin Italian blue cheese with a sharp, pungent, creamy flavour), Tiroler Speck (a cold-smoked and dry-cured pork product from the South Tyrol region of northern Italy with a smoky, salty, slightly sweet character), caramelized onions (slow-cooked to develop natural sweetness), tomato sauce, and mozzarella cheese, all baked on a thin wheat-flour crust. The combination produces a complex flavour profile: the bold umami and saltiness of the blue cheese and cured meat are balanced by the sweetness of the caramelized onions and the mild creaminess of the mozzarella. Nutritionally, this pizza is energy-dense (~285 kcal/100 g), high in protein (~13.5 g/100 g) and fat (~13.8 g/100 g, of which ~6.8 g is saturated), and provides a moderate carbohydrate load (~27.5 g/100 g) primarily from the dough. Sodium content is high (~680 mg/100 g), making a full serving a significant sodium source. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low-moderate, analogous to cheese pizza), but the glycemic load per full serving is substantial. Clinically, this dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and glycemic load. It is not suitable for halal or kosher diets due to the pork content, and contains gluten and dairy allergens. This dish is served at Italian restaurants in Singapore, including Ricciotti at The Riverwalk.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Gorgonzola e Speck pizza. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza with cheese as the base, with ingredient-level adjustments for Gorgonzola DOP and Tiroler Speck. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved source (cheese pizza, University of Sydney GI Database). Confidence is medium because the adaptation is methodologically sound but not an exact match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and ingredient-level data; no exact match exists for this specific pizza variant. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this pizza type in the USDA source database. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza entry used as the base. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the composite ingredient sources used. 5. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate based on cheese pizza (thin crust) from the University of Sydney GI Database; actual GI may be slightly lower due to the fat-attenuating effect of Gorgonzola and Speck. 6. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 27.5 × 3.0) / 100 = 41.3 g for a 300 g serving. 7. This pizza contains pork (Speck) and is not halal or kosher. 8. The food_group 'Grains and staples' is preserved from the input as pizza is dough-based; food_subgroup refined to 'Pizza'. 9. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian dish served in Singapore; no Singaporean-cuisine multilingual requirement applies as this is an Italian cuisine item. 10. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input is incorrect for this pizza and has been disregarded.
